stem: A drug candidate enters the drug development pipeline. The probability of passing preclinical testing is 88%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing Phase I trial is 85%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing Phase II trial is between 61% and 76% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the drug candidate is approved for market?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved for market) ranges from 0.456 to 0.568 as the Phase II trial pass rate varies from 61% to 76%. Point estimate: 0.516.

solution_steps: The drug candidate must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the Phase II trial stage creates a 11.2 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the Phase II trial rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
